title: antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. graniticum
intro: Antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. graniticum, also known as the Granitic Toadflax, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the family Plantaginaceae and is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be attributed to its high concentration of iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Antirrhinum hispanicum subsp. graniticum include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an antiseptic to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in the form of a cough syrup or expectorant to help relieve respiratory congestion.
